message:
test(conformance): differential harness for sys/socket.h vs glibc

CONFORMANCE: libc sys/socket.h diff matrix.

7 functions covered: socket / bind / listen / setsockopt / getsockopt
/ getsockname / shutdown / socketpair (8 in spec, but bind+listen are
exercised together as a setup pair).

Tests:
- socket_create_close: 7 (domain, type, proto) tuples — AF_INET/INET6/
  UNIX × STREAM/DGRAM × CLOEXEC|NONBLOCK; plus invalid_domain probe
- bind_listen_loopback: SO_REUSEADDR, bind to 127.0.0.1 with port=0
  (kernel-assigned), listen(16); both impls succeed
- sockopt_roundtrip: SO_REUSEADDR/SO_KEEPALIVE/SO_RCVBUF/SO_SNDBUF
  set then get, post-set values must match
- getsockname_after_bind: family must be AF_INET, port must be a
  non-zero ephemeral
- shutdown_invalid_fd: EBADF match
- socketpair_unix_stream: rc + both fds non-negative

Avoids actual network I/O — focuses on the create / bind / option
contract under deterministic loopback inputs. 0 divergences observed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
